page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, ensuring predictable processing performance without hyperthreading overhead. The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-CPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for persistent data.

Operating with a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this instance is structured for compute-focused workloads that do not demand massive memory footprints. The dedicated CPU allocation provides stable execution compared to shared-core alternatives. This resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for lightweight compute tasks, batch processing, microservices, and basic web serving where dedicated CPU execution is required without the overhead of excess memory.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.s3.large is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of system memory. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance features 4 physical cores with a single thread per core, providing predictable compute performance without hyperthreading. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the ecs.s3.large is designed for compute-focused workloads that require dedicated CPU allocation but have modest memory requirements. This configuration offers a balanced, cost-efficient profile for tasks such as lightweight web serving, microservices, batch processing, and utility workloads.
